steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence shifts from a fact (poisonous amphibians migrate during the day) to a mechanism (warning colors deter daytime predators). The transition must link the daytime context to that explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — However,: signals a contradiction, but the second sentence explains rather than contradicts the first. ✗
  • Choice B — At that time,: means ‘in those circumstances,’ tying the daytime setting to how the amphibians survive in it. ✓
  • Choice C — In other words,: restates an idea rather than explaining a mechanism. ✗
  • Choice D — For example,: introduces an example, not a cause-and-effect explanation. ✗
B is correct.
